recommendations.headline: Stay in Ujjain if Mahakal Darshan is your top priority
recommendations.description: Reduce early-morning transfers, stay closer to the sacred circuit and keep more energy for darshan.

recommendations.headline: Stay in Indore for hotels, airport access, food and day trips
recommendations.description: Choose greater hotel variety and city comfort while accepting a daily Ujjain commute.

recommendations.headline: Stay in Bhopal for Sanchi, Bhimbetka and a heritage circuit
recommendations.description: Use Bhopal as a separate base in a longer Madhya Pradesh itinerary, not as a daily Ujjain commute.

comparison.idealVisitor: Families with parents, first-time pilgrims and visitors with limited days
Working professionals, international visitors and comfort-focused families

cons: Too far for daily Mahakal visits
comparison.idealVisitor: Travellers building a 6–8 day Madhya Pradesh itinerary

areas.name: Near Mahakal
areas.note: Most convenient, but busiest and often costliest during peak dates.

areas.name: Freeganj
areas.note: A practical central area with broader everyday services.

areas.name: Nanakheda
areas.note: Useful for highway access and newer accommodation options.

areas.name: Vijay Nagar
areas.note: Wide accommodation choice, malls, restaurants and easier business-travel comfort.

areas.name: Airport Road
areas.note: Prioritises airport convenience; allow time for Ujjain transfers.

areas.name: Sarafa / Central Indore
areas.note: Best for atmosphere and food exploration, with busier streets.

areas.name: New Market
areas.note: A lively central base with food and city transport nearby.

areas.name: MP Nagar
areas.note: Practical hotels, transport connections and everyday services.

areas.name: Lake View areas
areas.note: Choose this for a calmer setting and scenic Bhopal experience.

quiz.question: Traveling with parents?
quiz.reason: Shorter travel to Mahakal saves energy and allows more rest.

quiz.question: Coming by flight?
quiz.reason: Indore has the nearest major airport and wider airport-transfer options.

quiz.question: Want food and shopping?
quiz.reason: Indore offers the strongest combination of dining, markets and modern retail.

quiz.question: Want a peaceful stay?
quiz.reason: Lake-view areas and a slower heritage circuit can provide a calmer extension.

quiz.question: Want lowest travel time to Mahakal?
quiz.reason: Staying locally removes the repeated intercity commute.

budgets.notes: Expect basic facilities and confirm family, bathroom and booking rules directly.
Check cleanliness reviews, lift access, hot water and vehicle approach.
Look for breakfast, power backup, lift access and flexible cancellation.
Indore usually provides the widest premium selection.
Confirm kitchen access, housekeeping, stairs and local transport before booking.

faqs.question: Is it better to stay in Ujjain or Indore for Mahakal Darshan?
faqs.answer: Stay in Ujjain when darshan convenience and lower daily travel matter most. Choose Indore for airport access, wider hotel choice and food, ideally by splitting nights between both cities.

faqs.question: Can we stay in Bhopal and visit Ujjain daily?
faqs.answer: Bhopal is generally too far for a comfortable daily Ujjain commute. Use it as a separate base for Sanchi, Bhimbetka and a longer Madhya Pradesh circuit.

faqs.question: Which Ujjain area is best for elderly visitors?
faqs.answer: Accommodation near Mahakal reduces transport, but confirm lift access, vehicle approach and crowd-control restrictions. Freeganj may offer a calmer balance for some families.

faqs.question: How early should Kumbh accommodation be booked?
faqs.answer: Book refundable accommodation as early as practical, then reconfirm access, cancellation and transport details when official arrangements are announced.

faqs.question: What should families check before booking a hotel?
faqs.answer: Check lift access, room size, extra beds, hot water, power backup, breakfast timing, cancellation terms and whether vehicles can reach the property during restrictions.
